meaning: adj.原始的,远古的,粗糙的,简单的
examples: Duffy's primitive guitar playing is well below par.
At the time, medicine was still a primitive quasi-science.
Primitive humans needed to be able to react like this to escape from dangerous animals.
In an era of high-speed recombinant genetic drugmaking, vaccine production is still primitive, little changed in 50 years.
It's a very evolutionarily hard-wired response that comes from a primitive need to respond very quickly.
